title: calluna vulgaris f. aurea
intro: Calluna vulgaris f. aurea, also known as the golden heather, is a variety of the calluna vulgaris plant, a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe, known for its vibrant yellow flowers and ability to thrive in acidic soils. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of calluna vulgaris f. aurea include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
